results.possibleIdentity: Pinchas Teitelbaum was a technical professional specializing in mathematics and physics, with expertise in both theory and application. During the late 1960s and early 1970s, he was based in New York City, specifically in the 10011 ZIP code area of Manhattan. Teitelbaum was an active member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to foster collaborations between the arts and technical fields. He was listed in the E.A.T. Technical Services directory, a program designed to match artists with engineers and scientists to solve technical problems in creative projects. Professional records indicate he was also a member or contributor to the American Mathematical Society (AMS), appearing in the society's notices as late as 1986.
